description: Keep installer-detected host defaults in gitignored local env instead of dirtying tracked harness.yaml.

userStories.title: Install without dirtying tracked config
userStories.description: As a new Open Harness user, I want install.sh to preserve host-detected defaults without editing tracked source so my checkout remains updateable.
acceptanceCriteria: scripts/install.sh no longer writes sandbox.name, sandbox.timezone, git.user_name, or git.user_email into harness.yaml
Generated .devcontainer/.env includes SANDBOX_NAME and, when detected, TZ, GIT_USER_NAME, and GIT_USER_EMAIL
Existing .devcontainer/.env files are preserved; reruns do not clobber existing secrets or user customizations
Generated values are quote-safe for shell sourcing when values contain spaces or apostrophes
userStories.notes: Implemented in scripts/install.sh; host defaults now write to gitignored .devcontainer/.env only when that file is generated.

userStories.title: Document local/default precedence clearly
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want generated and user-facing docs to explain where host-local defaults live so installer behavior matches docs.
acceptanceCriteria: Generated .devcontainer/.env header says host-local defaults live there and harness.yaml remains clean
.devcontainer/.example.env describes .env as local defaults plus secrets, not secrets-only
README, quickstart docs, and installer completion text describe .devcontainer/.env as gitignored host defaults plus secrets
Existing precedence remains unchanged: active harness.yaml keys override .devcontainer/.env
userStories.notes: Updated installer output, README, docs/quickstart.md, and .devcontainer/.example.env.

userStories.title: Prevent regression
userStories.description: As an orchestrator, I want test coverage that catches tracked-config mutation in the installer before it ships.
acceptanceCriteria: A Vitest regression checks scripts/install.sh keeps host defaults out of tracked harness.yaml and preserves an existing .devcontainer/.env
The existing compose helper wiring test still verifies scripts/install.sh uses scripts/docker-compose.sh
pnpm exec vitest run scripts/__tests__/compose-args.test.ts passes
userStories.notes: Added coverage to scripts/__tests__/compose-args.test.ts; targeted verification passes.
